reasoning_chain_text: 1. For school years 2006-2007, 2007-2008, 2008-2009, 2009-2010, and 2010-2011, identify the NYC high schools ranked in the top eight by overall score and keep the school that appears in every year.
2. Determine the neighborhood named by the school that remains after the yearly comparison.
3. For that neighborhood, identify the person who built the first bridge connecting New York City to it.
4. Determine the province or area from which that bridge builder emigrated.
original_final_question: Can you first find the neighborhood after which the only NYC high school that was in the top 8 by overall score every year from 2006-2010 is named, then report which province did the person who built the first bridge connecting NYC to that neighborhood emigrate from?. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
original_reasoning_chain: Node 1: Find top 8 NYC high schools by overall score in 2006-07 → includes Marble Hill
Node 2: Find top 8 NYC high schools by overall score in 2007-08 → includes Marble Hill
Node 3: Find top 8 NYC high schools by overall score in 2008-09 → includes Marble Hill
Node 4: Find top 8 NYC high schools by overall score in 2009-10 → includes Marble Hill
Node 5: Find top 8 NYC high schools by overall score in 2010-11 → includes Marble Hill
Intersection of <node_1>, <node_2>, <node_3>, <node_4>, <node_5>: Only school in top 7 all five years → Marble Hill High School for International Studies
Node 6 depends on intersection: Find who built first bridge at Marble Hill in 1693 → Frederick Philipse
Node 7 depends on node 6: Find area of Netherlands where Philipse emigrated from → Friesland
